description: The BMS shall monitor each cell voltage against the overvoltage threshold. BMS_CellOV_Detect shall be set to 1 when any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_Threshold.

analysis_plan.description: Test the normal operation where BMS_CellOV_Detect is set to 1 when any cell voltage reaches or exceeds r_CellOV_Threshold.
Test the boundary condition where a cell voltage exactly equals r_CellOV_Threshold, ensuring BMS_CellOV_Detect triggers correctly.
Simulate a fault scenario where multiple cells exceed r_CellOV_Threshold simultaneously to verify proper handling and response by the BMS.
Verify that BMS_CellOV_Detect remains 1 after it has been triggered, indicating continuous monitoring of overvoltage conditions.
Ensure that the status of BMS_CellOV_Detect can be observed and logged for further analysis or diagnostic purposes.

cases.title: BMS Cell Overvoltage Detection - Normal Operation
cases.objective: To verify that BMS_CellOV_Detect is correctly set to 1 when any cell voltage reaches or exceeds the overvoltage threshold (r_CellOV_Threshold).
cases.precondition: The battery pack is fully initialized, and all cells are within normal operating conditions.
cases.postcondition: The BMS continues monitoring all cells, and BMS_CellOV_Detect remains at 1 for the cell that exceeded the threshold until it returns below the threshold or a reset occurs.

steps.action: Set one of the cell voltages to a value equal to or greater than r_CellOV_Threshold.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Detect shall be set to 1.

cases.title: BMS Cell Overvoltage Detection at Threshold Value
cases.objective: To verify that BMS_CellOV_Detect is set to 1 when any cell voltage equals the overvoltage threshold r_CellOV_Threshold.
cases.precondition: The BMS is initialized and all cells are within normal operating conditions. The cell voltage of one or more cells is exactly equal to r_CellOV_Threshold.
cases.postcondition: The BMS continues monitoring all cells, and the state of BMS_CellOV_Detect remains unchanged unless the voltage changes.

steps.action: Set the voltage of a specific cell (e.g., Cell 1) to the threshold value r_CellOV_Threshold.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Detect shall be set to 1 for that cell.

cases.title: Verify BMS response to simultaneous overvoltage on multiple cells
cases.objective: To ensure that BMS_CellOV_Detect is set to 1 when any cell voltage >= r_CellOV_Threshold for multiple cells.
cases.precondition: BMS is initialized and all cells are within normal operating range. r_CellOV_Threshold = [NEEDS REVIEW].
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Detect remains at 1, indicating that the overvoltage condition persists until the voltage returns below r_CellOV_Threshold.

steps.action: Simulate a scenario where the voltages of three cells (Cell1, Cell2, Cell3) exceed r_CellOV_Threshold simultaneously.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Detect is set to 1 for all three cells. The BMS should trigger an overvoltage warning or fault response as defined in the system specifications.

cases.title: Verify BMS_CellOV_Detect remains 1 after triggering due to cell voltage exceeding the overvoltage threshold
cases.objective: To verify that BMS_CellOV_Detect stays at 1 when any cell voltage exceeds r_CellOV_Threshold, indicating continuous monitoring of overvoltage conditions.
cases.precondition: The system is powered on and all cells are within normal operating parameters. The initial value of BMS_CellOV_Detect is 0.
cases.postcondition: The system state indicates continuous monitoring of the overvoltage condition, with BMS_CellOV_Detect remaining at 1 until the voltage returns to normal levels or a reset command is issued by the BMS.

steps.action: Set the voltage of one or more cells to exceed r_CellOV_Threshold, triggering BMS_CellOV_Detect to become 1.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Detect becomes 1. The BMS should log an overvoltage event and continue monitoring cell voltages.

steps.action: Wait for [NEEDS REVIEW] seconds after the initial trigger.
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Increase or maintain the voltage of one or more cells to exceed r_CellOV_Threshold.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Detect remains 1. The BMS should continue logging overvoltage events and monitoring cell voltages without resetting BMS_CellOV_Detect.

cases.title: Verify BMS response to overvoltage condition on a single cell
cases.objective: To ensure that BMS_CellOV_Detect is set to 1 when any cell voltage reaches the overvoltage threshold, verifying observability of the status.
cases.precondition: The system is powered up and all cells are within normal operating conditions. The overvoltage threshold (r_CellOV_Threshold) has been defined in the system parameters.
cases.postcondition: The system state remains unchanged, but the BMS_CellOV_Detect status is logged with a value of 1 for the affected cell, indicating an overvoltage condition has been detected.

steps.action: Apply a voltage to one cell that exceeds the predefined r_CellOV_Threshold value.
steps.expected: The BMS shall set BMS_CellOV_Detect to 1 for the affected cell and log this status change. The BMS should also trigger any necessary alerts or notifications if configured.

description:
IF  BMS_CellOV_Detect = 1 for a continuous duration >= t_CellOV_Debounce
THEN
  · BMS_CellOV_Flag := 1
  · Store the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID in the fault record

analysis_plan.description: Test the normal behavior where BMS_CellOV_Detect remains high for a duration equal to t_CellOV_Debounce, resulting in BMS_CellOV_Flag being set to 1 and fault record updates.
Test the boundary condition where BMS_CellOV_Detect is high exactly for the duration of t_CellOV_Debounce, ensuring BMS_CellOV_Flag is correctly triggered.
Test the threshold just below t_CellOV_Debounce to ensure BMS_CellOV_Flag does not trigger.
Test the threshold just above t_CellOV_Debounce to ensure BMS_CellOV_Flag triggers correctly.
Test the state transition where BMS_CellOV_Detect goes from 0 to 1 and back within a duration less than t_CellOV_Debounce, ensuring no false triggering of BMS_CellOV_Flag.
Verify that the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID are correctly stored in the fault record when BMS_CellOV_Detect is high for at least t_CellOV_Debounce.

cases.title: Verify BMS_CellOV_Flag is set to 1 and fault record updated when BMS_CellOV_Detect remains high for t_CellOV_Debounce duration
cases.objective: To verify the normal behavior where BMS_CellOV_Flag is correctly set to 1 and the maximum cell voltage along with affected cell ID are stored in the fault record, following a continuous BMS_CellOV_Detect signal of [NEEDS REVIEW] seconds.
cases.precondition: BMS_CellOV_Detect = 0; BMS_CellOV_Flag = 0; Fault records are cleared
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1; Fault records contain the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID from the test period.

steps.action: Set BMS_CellOV_Detect to 1 for a duration of [NEEDS REVIEW] seconds.
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Wait for [NEEDS REVIEW] seconds after setting BMS_CellOV_Detect to 1.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Flag := 1; Store the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID in the fault record

cases.title: Test BMS Cell Overvoltage Flag Trigger with Boundary Duration
cases.objective: To verify that BMS_CellOV_Flag is set to 1 when BMS_CellOV_Detect remains high for exactly the duration of t_CellOV_Debounce.
cases.precondition: BMS is in normal operation, and no overvoltage conditions have been detected previously.
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag is set to 1, and the maximum cell voltage along with the affected cell ID are stored in the fault record. BMS_CellOV_Detect returns to 0.

steps.action: Set BMS_CellOV_Detect to 1 at time t0.
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Wait for a duration of exactly t_CellOV_Debounce from t0 (t0 + t_CellOV_Debounce).
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Flag := 1; Store the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID in the fault record.

cases.title: Test BMS Cell Overvoltage Flag with Duration Just Below Debounce Time
cases.objective: To verify that BMS_CellOV_Flag is not triggered when BMS_CellOV_Detect remains active for a duration just below t_CellOV_Debounce.
cases.precondition: BMS initialization complete, no cell overvoltage detected currently (BMS_CellOV_Detect = 0).
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Detect returns to 0, no flag set, and no new entries in the fault record related to overvoltage.

steps.action: Simulate a cell overvoltage condition such that BMS_CellOV_Detect := 1 for a duration of [NEEDS REVIEW] seconds.
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Wait for [NEEDS REVIEW - t_CellOV_Debounce + 1] seconds, ensuring the duration is just below t_CellOV_Debounce.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Flag := 0; BMS does not store maximum cell voltage or affected cell ID in fault record.

cases.title: Test BMS Cell Overvoltage Flag Trigger at Boundary Duration
cases.objective: To verify that BMS_CellOV_Flag is set when BMS_CellOV_Detect remains high for a duration equal to t_CellOV_Debounce.
cases.precondition: BMS_CellOV_Detect = 1 and the system has been running without any other faults.
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag is set to 1, and the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID are stored in the fault record.

steps.action: Set BMS_CellOV_Detect to 1 and maintain this state for exactly t_CellOV_Debounce duration.
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Wait for the duration of t_CellOV_Debounce after setting BMS_CellOV_Detect to 1.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Flag := 1; Store the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID in the fault record.

cases.title: Test BMS Cell Overvoltage Flag Transition with Short Duration
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS does not trigger BMS_CellOV_Flag when BMS_CellOV_Detect transitions from 0 to 1 and back within t_CellOV_Debounce duration.
cases.precondition: BMS is in normal operation, all cell voltages are within safe limits, and no previous overvoltage events have been recorded.
cases.postcondition: BMS is back in a normal state with all cells within safe limits and no overvoltage events recorded.

steps.action: Set BMS_CellOV_Detect to 0 for all cells.
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Increase the voltage of a single cell above the overvoltage threshold (e.g., set it to [NEEDS REVIEW] V) and maintain this condition for t_CellOV_Debounce - 1 seconds.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Flag remains at 0, BMS does not store any fault record.

steps.action: Decrease the voltage of the cell back to within the safe limit over a period less than or equal to 1 second.
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Wait for t_CellOV_Debounce seconds after the cell voltage returns to normal.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Flag remains at 0, no fault record is stored.

cases.title: Verify storage of maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID in fault record during continuous BMS_CellOV_Detect
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS correctly stores the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID in the fault record when BMS_CellOV_Detect is high for a duration >= t_CellOV_Debounce.
cases.precondition: BMS is initialized, all cells are within normal operating range, and no previous OVP faults have been recorded.
cases.postcondition: BMS is in a state where it has recorded an OVP fault with the correct maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID stored in the fault record.

steps.action: Simulate an overvoltage condition on one or more cells such that BMS_CellOV_Detect = 1 for a duration of at least t_CellOV_Debounce [NEEDS REVIEW].
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Observe the BMS_CellOV_Flag to ensure it is set to 1.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Flag := 1

steps.action: Check the fault record for the maximum cell voltage and affected cell ID.
steps.expected: The fault record should contain the maximum cell voltage of the affected cells and their respective IDs.

description:
IF  BMS_CellOV_Detect de-asserts before t_CellOV_Debounce expires
THEN
  · The debounce timer shall reset
  · BMS_CellOV_Flag shall remain 0
  · No fault record shall be stored

analysis_plan.description: Test that the debounce timer resets and BMS_CellOV_Flag remains 0 when BMS_CellOV_Detect de-asserts before t_CellOV_Debounce expires.
Verify normal behavior where BMS_CellOV_Detect does not de-assert before t_CellOV_Debounce expires, ensuring the flag and timer behave as expected.
Test the boundary condition where BMS_CellOV_Detect de-asserts exactly at t_CellOV_Debounce to ensure proper handling of this threshold.

cases.title: Verify debounce timer reset and BMS_CellOV_Flag remains 0 when BMS_CellOV_Detect de-asserts before t_CellOV_Debounce
cases.objective: To verify that the debounce timer resets, BMS_CellOV_Flag remains 0, and no fault record is stored when BMS_CellOV_Detect de-asserts before t_CellOV_Debounce expires.
cases.precondition: BMS_CellOV_Detect is asserted, BMS_CellOV_Flag is set to 1, and the debounce timer has started but not yet expired (t < t_CellOV_Debounce).
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag is reset to 0, the debounce timer has been reset, and there are no fault records stored.

steps.action: Assert BMS_CellOV_Detect.
steps.expected: BMS_CellOV_Flag is set to 1; no fault record is stored.

steps.action: De-assert BMS_CellOV_Detect before t_CellOV_Debounce expires (t < t_CellOV_Debounce).
steps.expected: The debounce timer resets, and BMS_CellOV_Flag remains 0; no fault record is stored.

cases.title: Verify debounce timer reset and flag state when BMS_CellOV_Detect does not de-assert before t_CellOV_Debounce expires
cases.objective: To verify that the debounce timer resets and BMS_CellOV_Flag remains 0 if BMS_CellOV_Detect does not de-assert within the debounce period, ensuring no fault record is stored.
cases.precondition: BMS_CellOV_Detect is asserted, t_CellOV_Debounce = [NEEDS REVIEW] ms
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Detect is de-asserted, debounce timer is reset, BMS_CellOV_Flag remains 0, no fault record is stored.

steps.action: Assert BMS_CellOV_Detect and wait for [NEEDS REVIEW] ms (t_CellOV_Debounce).
steps.expected: None

steps.action: De-assert BMS_CellOV_Detect before the end of t_CellOV_Debounce.
steps.expected: The debounce timer shall reset, and BMS_CellOV_Flag shall remain 0. No fault record shall be stored.

cases.title: Test for boundary condition where BMS_CellOV_Detect de-asserts exactly at t_CellOV_Debounce
cases.objective: To verify that the debounce timer resets and no fault is recorded when BMS_CellOV_Detect de-asserts exactly at t_CellOV_Debounce, as per REQ-BMS-OVP-003.
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag = 0, debounce timer is reset, no fault recorded in the system.

steps.action: Assert BMS_CellOV_Detect and wait for t_CellOV_Debounce [NEEDS REVIEW] seconds
steps.expected: None

steps.action: De-assert BMS_CellOV_Detect at exactly t_CellOV_Debounce [NEEDS REVIEW] seconds
steps.expected: The debounce timer shall reset, and BMS_CellOV_Flag shall remain 0. No fault record shall be stored.

description:
The following data shall be stored in the fault record upon BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1:
  · Peak cell voltage and affected cell ID
  · Pack temperature at time of fault
  · Event timestamp

analysis_plan.description: Verify that the system correctly stores peak cell voltage, affected cell ID, and pack temperature in the fault record when BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1.

cases.title: Verify storage of peak cell voltage, affected cell ID, and pack temperature in fault record upon BMS_CellOV_Flag activation
cases.objective: To verify that the system correctly stores peak cell voltage, affected cell ID, and pack temperature in the fault record when BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1.
cases.precondition: BMS is initialized and operational. BMS_CellOV_Flag is set to 0 initially.
cases.postcondition: BMS continues to operate normally, with the fault record containing the recorded data points from step 2 and step 3.

steps.action: Set BMS_CellOV_Flag to 1 manually or through a simulation tool, indicating an overvoltage condition in one of the cells.
Observe the system and confirm that the peak cell voltage and affected cell ID are stored in the fault record.
steps.expected: The BMS should store the peak cell voltage and the affected cell ID in the fault record.

steps.action: Check the pack temperature at the time of the fault occurrence and confirm that it is also stored in the fault record.
steps.expected: The BMS should store the pack temperature at the time of the fault in the fault record.

description:
Upon BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, the BMS shall:
  · Broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180 at 100 ms periodicity
  · Reduce charge current target to 50 % of nominal

analysis_plan.description: Verify that BMS_CellOV_Warn is broadcasted on CAN ID 0x180 with a periodicity of 100ms when BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions to 1.
Test the behavior at the threshold where BMS_CellOV_Flag changes from 0 to 1, ensuring that BMS_CellOV_Warn is correctly broadcasted and charge current target is reduced to 50% of nominal.

Simulate a fault scenario where BMS_CellOV_Flag remains at 1 for an extended period (e.g., 2 seconds) to ensure the system handles prolonged overvoltage conditions correctly, including continued broadcasting and charge current reduction.
Verify that when BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions back from 1 to 0, BMS_CellOV_Warn stops being broadcasted and charge current target returns to nominal value.

cases.title: Verify BMS_CellOV_Warn broadcasting and charge current reduction upon BMS_CellOV_Flag transition
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS broadcasts BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180 at a periodicity of 100ms when BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions to 1, and reduces the charge current target to 50% of nominal.
cases.precondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag is currently 0. The system is in normal operation with no cell overvoltage detected.
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag remains 1, and the charge current target is reduced to 50% of nominal. BMS_CellOV_Warn continues to be broadcasted at a periodicity of 100ms on CAN ID 0x180.

steps.action: Set BMS_CellOV_Flag to 1 manually or through the simulation environment.
Wait for 100ms.
steps.expected: The BMS shall broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180.

steps.action: Wait for an additional 100ms (total of 200ms from step 1).
steps.expected: The BMS shall continue to broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180.

steps.action: Wait for an additional 100ms (total of 300ms from step 1).
steps.expected: The BMS shall continue to broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180.

cases.title: BMS responds correctly when BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions from 0 to 1
cases.objective: To verify the BMS broadcasts BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180 at 100 ms periodicity and reduces charge current target to 50% of nominal upon BMS_CellOV_Flag transitioning from 0 to 1.
cases.precondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag is currently set to 0, BMS_CellOV_Warn = 0, and the charge current target is at its nominal value.
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag is set to 1, BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180 with a periodicity of 100 ms, and the charge current target is at 50% of its nominal value.

steps.action: Set BMS_CellOV_Flag to 1.
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Wait for 100 ms.
steps.expected: Broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180.

steps.action: Wait for another 100 ms (total of 200 ms).
steps.expected: Broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180 again.

steps.action: Check the charge current target value after a sufficient duration to ensure it has been reduced to 50% of nominal.
steps.expected: The charge current target should be at 50% of its nominal value.

cases.title: Verify BMS response to prolonged BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1 condition
cases.objective: To verify that the BMS broadcasts BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 on CAN ID 0x180 at 100 ms periodicity and reduces charge current target to 50% of nominal when BMS_CellOV_Flag remains at 1 for an extended period (e.g., 2 seconds).
cases.precondition: BMS is powered up, BMS_CellOV_Flag = 0, and the system is in a normal operating state.
cases.postcondition: BMS is in a state where BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, BMS_CellOV_Warn is being broadcasted on CAN ID 0x180 at 100 ms periodicity, and charge current target is set to 50% of nominal.

steps.action: Simulate BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1 by applying an overvoltage condition to one or more cells.
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Wait for 2 seconds with BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1.
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Verify that BMS_CellOV_Warn is broadcasted on CAN ID 0x180 at 100 ms periodicity during the 2-second period.
steps.expected: The BMS shall broadcast BMS_CellOV_Warn = 1 every 100 ms on CAN ID 0x180.

steps.action: Verify that charge current target is reduced to 50% of nominal during the 2-second period with BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1.
steps.expected: The BMS shall reduce the charge current target to 50% of its nominal value while BMS_CellOV_Flag remains at 1.

cases.title: Verify BMS behavior upon transition of BMS_CellOV_Flag from 1 to 0
cases.objective: To verify that when BMS_CellOV_Flag transitions back from 1 to 0, BMS_CellOV_Warn stops being broadcasted and charge current target returns to nominal value.
cases.precondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag = 1, BMS_CellOV_Warn is broadcasting on CAN ID 0x180 at 100 ms periodicity, and the charge current target has been reduced to 50% of nominal.
cases.postcondition: BMS_CellOV_Flag = 0, BMS_CellOV_Warn is not broadcasting on CAN ID 0x180, and the charge current target has returned to its nominal value.

steps.action: Set BMS_CellOV_Flag = 0
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Wait for 1 second (to ensure any ongoing broadcasts complete)
steps.expected: None

steps.action: Check CAN bus for BMS_CellOV_Warn messages on ID 0x180
steps.expected: No BMS_CellOV_Warn messages should be received.

steps.action: Check the charge current target value in the BMS software or simulation environment
steps.expected: The charge current target should return to its nominal value.
